zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Dudley於1488年8月出生在英國素可時，她的父親Edmund Dudley Esq.16歲，母親Anne Windsor22歲。她於大約1518年與Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton在英國結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1560年8月19日在英國維爾爾斯圖頓享年72歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾杜德利聖 艾德蒙達斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Dudley于1488年8月出生在英国素可时，她的父亲Edmund Dudley Esq.16岁，母亲Anne Windsor22岁。她于大约1518年与Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton在英国结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1560年8月19日在英国維爾爾斯圖頓享年72岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾杜德利聖 艾德蒙達斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Dudley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands): habitational name from Dudley in Worcestershire, recorded as Duddeleye, Doddeleye in the 13th and 14th centuries, named from the Old English personal name Dudda (see Dodd 1) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish (southern Ireland): in Ireland, when not the English name, it was adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Dubhdáleithe ‘descendant of Dubhdáleithe’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + dá ‘two’ + léithe ‘sides’.

variants.name: Dowdall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish John Patrick, Niall.
Irish (Dublin, Meath, and Louth): English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Dubhdáleithe (see Dudley 2).

variants.name: Dagley
meanings: English: variant of Dadley, itself a variant of Dudley , with dissimilation of d-d to d-g.
Americanized form of German Dägele (see Dagle ) or Degele, from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with Old High German diot ‘people, nation’.

variants.name: Dodd
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Dodd(e), Dudd(e), Old English Dodd(a), Dudd(a), a name of uncertain origin which remained in fairly widespread and frequent use from Lincolnshire to Devon and from Essex to Lancashire in England until the 14th century.

English: nickname from Middle English dod, a word of uncertain meaning, possibly a ‘lumpish, thickset person’ (compare modern English dialect dod ‘bunch or heap’), or by extension a ‘foolish person’ (compare Middle English dode-mused ‘stupid’), or perhaps a derivative of dodden ‘to shave (the head), to trim (hair)’, hence ‘the hairless or close-cropped one’.

English: possibly a modern variant of Daud or Dowd, the former arising from the Middle English personal name Daud(e), an extended form of Daw , and the latter from the Middle English personal name Doude, perhaps a side-form of either Dodde or of Daude, a pet form of Ralph .

variants.name: Diddle
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Dudley  . Compare   Aduddell  .
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of German   Dittel  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Dudley was born in August 1488, in Sussex, England, her father, Edmund Dudley Esq., was 16 and her mother, Anne Windsor, was 22. She married Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton about 1518, in England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 19 August 1560, in Stourton, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Dudley est née en août 1488, à Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Dudley Esq., avait 16 ans et sa mère, Anne Windsor, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton environ 1518, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 août 1560, à Stourton, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcester: Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Dudley im August 1488 in Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Dudley Esq. 16 und ihre Mutter Anne Windsor 22. Sie hat um 1518 in England Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 19. August 1560 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Stourton, Wiltshire, England und wurde in Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, England begraben.

Lei sposato Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton circa 1518, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 19 agosto 1560, nel Stourton, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolta a Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Dudleyが1488年8月にイギリススセックスで生まれた時，父Edmund Dudley Esq.は16歳，母Anne Windsorは22歳であった。約1518年にイギリスでSir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1560年8月19日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアストウルトンで72歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアダドリーサイント エドムンド'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcester: 영국 워스스터실르 드들리 세인트 에드먼드'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Dudley(이)가 1488년 8월 영국 수세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Dudley Esq.(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Windsor(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1518년 영국에서 Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1560년 8월 19일 영국 윌츠히르 스토우르턴에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 드들리 세인트 에드먼드'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Dudley nasceu em agosto de 1488, em Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Dudley Esq., tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Anne Windsor, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton aproximadamente 1518, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 19 de agosto de 1560, em Stourton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcester: сеинт эдмунд'с чурч, дудли, ворсестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Dudley родилась август 1488 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Dudley Esq. было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Windsor, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton около 1518 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 19 августа 1560 г. в местности стоуртон, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт эдмунд'с чурч, дудли, ворсестиршир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir William Stourton 7th Baron Stourton aproximadamente 1518, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 19 de agosto de 1560, en Stourton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Saint Edmund's Church, Dudley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
